     The Numbers Business Report

Victoria and Abdul (2017)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $22,245,070Details
International Box Office $46,026,076
Worldwide Box Office $68,271,146
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $1,133,192
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $299,764
Total $1,432,956
Further financial details...

Synopsis

The true story of an unexpected friendship in the later years of Queen Victoria’s remarkable rule. When Abdul Karim, a young clerk, travels from India to participate in the Queen’s Golden Jubilee, he is surprised to find favor with the Queen herself. As the Queen questions the constrictions of her long-held position, the two forge an unlikely and devoted alliance with a loyalty to one another that her household and inner circle all attempt to destroy. As the friendship deepens, the Queen begins to see a changing world through new eyes and joyfully reclaims her humanity.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$158,845 (0.7% of total gross)
Legs:5.33 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:32.6% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$21,000,000 (worldwide box office is 3.3 times production budget)
Theater counts:4 opening theaters/1,060 max. theaters, 5.7 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $29,659,855

Full financial estimates for this film, including domestic and international box office, video sales, video rentals, TV and ancillary revenue are available through our research services. For more information, please contact us at research@the-numbers.com.


  • Summary
  • Box Office
  • Full Financials

Synopsis

The true story of an unexpected friendship in the later years of Queen Victoria’s remarkable rule. When Abdul Karim, a young clerk, travels from India to participate in the Queen’s Golden Jubilee, he is surprised to find favor with the Queen herself. As the Queen questions the constrictions of her long-held position, the two forge an unlikely and devoted alliance with a loyalty to one another that her household and inner circle all attempt to destroy. As the friendship deepens, the Queen begins to see a changing world through new eyes and joyfully reclaims her humanity.
